zh.Ashburnham, Sussex, England: 英國素可阿申波恩哈姆

nameMeanings.name: Beching
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch and German: farm name, mainly in the border region of East Netherlands (Overijssel) and Germany (North Rhine-Westphalia), indicating its location ‘beside a stream’ (see   Beck   6).

variants.name: Beck
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from northern Middle English bekke ‘stream’ (Old Norse bekkr).

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named Bec, for example Bec Hellouin in Eure, named with Old Norman French bec ‘stream’, from the same Old Norse root as in 1 above.
English: from the Middle English personal name Becke (Old English Becca or Beocca), of uncertain origin.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching was born in 1520, in Ashford, Kent, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Priscilla Gifford. He died on 2 August 1593, in Ashburnham, Sussex, England, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching est né en 1520, à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Priscilla Gifford. Il est décédé le 2 août 1593, à Ashburnham, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching wurde 1520 in Ashford, Kent, England geboren. Er hatte mit Priscilla Gifford mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 2. August 1593 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Ashburnham, Sussex, England.

it.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching è nato il nel 1520 nel Ashford, Kent, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Priscilla Gifford. Morì il 2 agosto 1593, nel Ashburnham, Sussex, England, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.Ashford, Kent, England: イギリスイギリスケントアシュフォード
ja.Ashburnham, Sussex, England: イギリススセックスアシュブルンハム
ja.lifeSummary: John Edward Bechingは1520年にイギリスケントアシュフォードで生まれた。彼はPriscilla Giffordとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1593年8月2日にイギリススセックスアシュブルンハムで73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching(은)는 1520년 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 태어났다. 그는 Priscilla Gifford(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1593년 8월 2일 영국 수세스 애스h번함에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching nasceu em 1520, em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Priscilla Gifford. Ele faleceu em 2 de agosto de 1593, em Ashburnham, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching родился в 1520 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Priscilla Gifford. Он умер 2 августа 1593 г. в местности ашбурнам, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: John Edward Beching nació en 1520, en Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Priscilla Gifford. Murió el 2 de agosto de 1593, en Ashburnham, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años.
